promoted_anchors: Fast AI coding systems do not mainly fail because of syntax mistakes. Their more dangerous failure mode is false confidence: - growing the wrong file - reviving the wrong pattern - trusting a stale seam - treating weak evidence like certainty - sounding more confident than the repository justifies Sherlog exists to expose these risks before code is grown.

Sherlog helps anyone shipping quickly with AI, from solo developers to teams. It is especially useful for vibe coders because they are most exposed to false confidence. If code is being generated faster than a mental model is being built, the main failure mode is not syntax. It is orientation failure: - landing-zone mistakes - misleading seams - stale context - unearned certainty When Sherlog is working correctly,...
